# Gcore Docs

Documentation for Gcore Cloud, CDN, DNS, Storage, FastEdge, and other products.
MCP Server: https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/integrations/gcore-mcp-server-overview.md
GitHub repo (raw MDX): https://github.com/G-Core/product-documentation

## Account settings

User profile and 2FA, account registration and KYC, billing cycles, payments, expenses, VAT, trial conditions, user roles and IAM, permanent API tokens, Gcore MCP Server installation and integration with Claude Code, Cursor IDE, and MCP Inspector, service notifications, support response times, status page subscriptions.

- [Account settings](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings.md)

## My profile
- [My profile settings](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/my-profile/overview.md): Configure username, email, language, two-factor authentication, password, and view login history in My profile settings for individual user accounts.
- [Set up two-factor authentication](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/my-profile/two-factor-authentication.md): Enable two-factor authentication in Gcore Customer Portal using authenticator apps (Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator, Authy) and QR code scanning.
- [Change or reset a password](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/my-profile/change-password.md): Update account password in Gcore Customer Portal Profile settings or reset forgotten password via email verification link.
- [What to do if you do not receive the reset email?](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/my-profile/password-reset-email.md): Check spam folder for "Password Recovery" email from support@gcore.com; retry password reset at auth.gcore.com/login/forgot-password if email not received wi...

## Account
- [Account settings](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/account/overview.md): Configure account identification, billing address, tax location, currency, and initiate KYC verification to switch from individual to company account status.
- [Create an account for an individual or legal entity](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/account/create-account.md): Register individual or legal entity accounts via Google, GitHub, or email; confirm registration and access Gcore Customer Portal dashboard.
- [Create an additional account and switch between accounts](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/account/additional-accounts.md): Create and switch between multiple accounts from a single login using the profile menu's Switch account and Create a new account options.
- [Disable or resume services](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/account/services.md): Disable, resume, or activate Gcore services via Customer Portal Services page with status tracking and suspension timelines.
- [Account deletion](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/account/account-deletion.md): Request permanent account deletion via Settings > Account > General information; suspended accounts with outstanding debt cannot be deleted until payment is ...

## Billing
- [Billing overview](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/billing/overview.md): Track consumption-based billing cycles, threshold comparisons, and Cloud Pay-As-You-Go tariff charges collected monthly with 5-minute intervals.
- [Expenses](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/billing/expenses.md): View consumed Gcore product costs by service, feature, usage, and pricing rule status (commitment/overcommitment) with tax and discount breakdowns.
- [Payments and payment methods](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/billing/payments.md): View payment history, transaction statuses (Paid, Unpaid, Pending, Refunded, Cancelled), and manage payment methods (credit card, debit card, PayPal, Google ...
- [Bonuses and discounts](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/billing/bonuses-and-discounts.md): View bonus credits and active discounts in the Billing section; track bonus transactions, expiration dates, and discount percentages by service and feature.
- [Trial conditions](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/billing/trial-conditions.md): Activate free trial periods for CDN, Video Streaming, Web Application Security, and Managed DNS Pro with specified duration and usage limits via Gcore Custom...
- [VAT rates in different countries for an individual and legal entity](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/billing/vat-rates.md): Reference VAT rates by country and user type (individual or organization), with conditional rates for EU organizations based on VAT number provision.
- [TAX location](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/billing/tax-location.md): Configure tax location in billing profile to determine applicable tax rates and VAT charges based on country regulations and payment method information.
- [Data deletion](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/billing/data-deletion.md): Delete suspended services and associated customer data after product-specific retention periods ranging from immediate deletion to 90 days for CDN, DNS, Vide...
- [payment-verification](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/billing/payment-verification.md)

## Users
- [About users](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/users/about-users.md): Define user roles (Administrator, Engineer, Purge and Prefetch) and permissions for CDN, Video Streaming, and account management in Gcore Customer Portal.
- [Invite, edit, and delete a user](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/users/manage-users.md): Invite users to Gcore Customer Portal with configurable roles for CDN, IAM, Edge Cloud, and other services; send invitations via email to registered or new u...
- [Create, use, or delete a permanent API token](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/api-tokens.md): Create, configure, and manage permanent API tokens with role-based access control, expiration settings, and APIKey authorization headers for automated CDN op...
- [Manage service notifications and promotional emails](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/notifications.md): Configure product notifications, technical alerts, and promotional emails per user or account-wide in Gcore Customer Portal Profile settings; set CDN and Obj...
- [How quickly our support team will respond to you](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/how-quickly-our-support-team-will-respond-to-you.md): Support response times depend on billing plan (free/medium/PRO+), request priority (P1–P4), and support team workload; set priority in Help Center for CDN, V...
- [Be aware of the service status, scheduled and emergency maintenance](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/be-aware-of-the-service-status-scheduled-and-emergency-maintenance.md): Subscribe to Gcore Status Page via email, Slack, or webhook to receive notifications about service outages, scheduled maintenance, and incident updates.
- [Roadmap: Provide feedback, suggest new features, follow development](https://gcore.com/docs/account-settings/roadmap-provide-feedback-suggest-new-features-follow-development.md): Submit feature requests and track development status across CDN, Managed DNS, Edge Cloud, Video Streaming, and Web Application Security products on Gcore Roa...

## CDN

CDN resources for site, static asset, and video acceleration; origins, origin groups, origin shielding, SSL/SNI; CMS integrations (WordPress, Drupal, Magento, Joomla, AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage); caching rules, cache key, sharding, stale-on-error; secure tokens, country/IP/referrer/user-agent access policies, TLS versions; Gzip and Brotli compression; HTTP headers and CORS; image optimization (WebP, AVIF, resize, crop); rules and templates; Grafana statistics; BYOIP, dedicated IP, prefetch, logs uploader, Let's Encrypt and custom SSL certificates; Terraform provider; troubleshooting status codes, redirects, cache hit ratio; Video CDN for HLS, MPEG-DASH, FLV with secure tokens.

- [CDN](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn.md)

## Getting Started

## Create a CDN resource
- [CDN resource overview](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/create-a-cdn-resource.md): Create CDN resources for entire sites, static assets, or video streaming to accelerate content delivery via Gcore edge servers and caching infrastructure.
- [Create a CDN resource for static assets](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/create-a-cdn-resource/create-a-cdn-resource-for-only-static-files.md): Configure CDN resource with custom domain, origin group, and CNAME record to accelerate static asset delivery.
- [Create a CDN resource for the entire site](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/create-a-cdn-resource/create-a-cdn-resource-for-the-entire-site.md): Create a CDN resource for entire site acceleration using Gcore CDN and Managed DNS to deliver static and dynamic content without code changes.
- [Create a CDN resource for video streaming](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/create-a-cdn-resource/create-a-cdn-resource-for-video-streaming.md): Quick guide to set up Video CDN for delivering HLS, DASH, or FLV live streams and VOD content

## Integrate CDN with CMS and other platforms
- [About Gcore CDN integration with CMS and other platforms](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms.md): Integrate Gcore CDN with CMS platforms (WordPress, Drupal, Magento, Joomla) and cloud storage (AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage) by replacing application domain ...
- [Integrate CDN resource with AWS S3](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-aws-s3.md): Configure Gcore CDN resource with AWS S3 bucket as origin, enable static website hosting, set bucket policy, and verify content delivery through CDN CNAME.
- [Integrate CDN resource with B2evolution](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-b2evolution.md): Configure B2evolution CMS to serve static assets through Gcore CDN by replacing base URL variables with CNAME in configuration file.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Cloudflare](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-clouldflare.md): Configure Gcore CDN CNAME records with Cloudflare DNS by disabling proxying and set Origin Pull Protocol to HTTP or add SNI Hostname to avoid 502 errors.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Concrete5](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-concrete5.md): Install Concrete5 CDN extensions and configure Gcore CDN resource CNAME to rewrite static file URLs through content delivery network.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Craft](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-craft.md): Configure Craft CMS asset sources with Gcore CDN CNAME to serve static files through CDN resource.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Cs-cart](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-cs-cart.md): Install Gcore CDN plugin for Cs-cart, configure CNAME in Universal CDN Add-on settings, and verify static asset URLs are rewritten to CDN domain.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Django CMS](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-django-cms.md): Configure Django CMS urls.py and settings.py to deliver static files via Gcore CDN using CNAME, then run collectstatic command.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Drupal](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-drupal.md): Install Gcore CDN Module in Drupal, configure Origin Pull mode with CNAME, enable Far Future expiration and HTTPS/SSL support.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Google Cloud Storage](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-google-cloud-storage.md): Configure Google Cloud Storage bucket as CDN origin, create CDN resource in Gcore portal, and verify CNAME record setup for content delivery.
- [Integrate CDN resource with IPB](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-ipb.md): Configure IPB CMS to deliver static assets through Gcore CDN by setting CNAME in System settings image, CSS, and JS URL fields.
- [Integrate CDN resource with IPS](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-ips.md): Configure IPS Community CDN integration by entering CNAME in System Settings General Configuration CDN Settings section.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Joomla](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-joomla.md): Install Gcore CDN plugin for Joomla 3.4.1+, configure CNAME in System - CDN plugin settings, verify static asset URLs rewritten to CDN domain.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Kirby](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-kirby.md): Configure Kirby CMS to use Gcore CDN by manually updating CSS/JS paths in header.php and using GetImages plugin for image delivery.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Magento](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-magento.md): Configure Magento Admin Panel System settings to integrate Gcore CDN resource using CNAME, merge JavaScript/CSS files, and set Base Skin URL for HTTP/HTTPS d...
- [Integrate CDN resource with Perch](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-perch.md): Configure Perch CMS to deliver static assets through Gcore CDN by updating configuration files with CDN resource CNAME and bucket paths.
- [Integrate CDN resource with PrestaShop](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-prestashop.md): Configure Gcore CDN CNAME in PrestaShop Advanced Parameters/Performance CCC settings and verify static asset URL rewriting via browser DevTools.
- [Integrate CDN resource with PyroCMS](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-pyrocms.md): Configure Gcore CDN CNAME integration in PyroCMS admin panel Settings to rewrite static asset URLs.
- [Integrate CDN resource with SocialEngine](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-socialengine.md): Configure Gcore CDN CNAME in SocialEngine admin panel Settings/General Settings and verify URL rewriting in browser Network tab.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Typo3](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-typo3.md): Install smile_cdn extension in Typo3 and configure Gcore CDN CNAME to rewrite asset URLs for content delivery.
- [Integrate CDN resource with vBulletin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-vbulletin.md): Configure vBulletin Replacement Variables to rewrite static asset URLs to Gcore CDN CNAME for content delivery integration.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Webasyst](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-webasyst.md): Configure Gcore CDN CNAME in Webasyst administration settings and verify URL rewriting in browser Network tab.

## Wordpress
- [Integrate CDN resource with WordPress (Gcore plugin)](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/wordpress/integrate-cdn-resource-with-wordpress-gcore-plugin.md): Install and configure Gcore CDN plugin for WordPress to enable CDN delivery with file type, folder path, and exception URL settings.
- [Integrate CDN resource with WordPress (Rocket plugin)](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/wordpress/integrate-cdn-resource-with-wordpress-rocket-plugin.md): Configure Gcore CDN with WordPress using WP Rocket plugin by setting CNAME and hostname replacement for static file delivery.
- [Integrate CDN resource with WordPress (W3 Total Cache plugin)](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/wordpress/integrate-cdn-resource-with-wordpress-w3-total-cache-plugin.md): Configure Gcore CDN with WordPress W3 Total Cache plugin by enabling Generic Mirror CDN type and entering CNAME hostname in Performance settings.
- [Integrate CDN resource with WordPress (WP SuperCache plugin)](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/wordpress/integrate-cdn-resource-with-wordpress-wp-supercache-plugin.md): Configure WP Super Cache plugin CDN tab with Gcore CNAME, enable CDN support, and verify static file URL rewriting in WordPress.
- [Integrate CDN resource with X-Cart](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-x-cart.md): Configure X-Cart 4.4.x+ to deliver static content via Gcore CDN by modifying smarty.php, func.files.php, and template files with CNAME records.
- [Integrate CDN resource with XenForo](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-xenforo.md): Configure XenForo CDN integration by editing config.php and updating Style image path to Gcore CNAME for static asset delivery.
- [Extend CDN functionality with FastEdge](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-fastedge.md): Integrate FastEdge serverless compute with CDN edge locations to extend content delivery with custom logic and request/response manipulation.

## Configure an origin
- [Add CDN servers to the origin ACL whitelist](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/configure-an-origin/add-cdn-servers-to-the-origin-acl-whitelist.md): Whitelist CDN edge servers on origin via HTTP headers, IP addresses, or subnets using API endpoints or request headers.
- [Сonfigure HTTP/2 on the origin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/configure-an-origin/configure-http-2-on-the-origin.md): Enable HTTP/2 protocol on origin servers using Nginx 1.9.5+ or Apache 2.4.17+ with mod_http2 module for improved CDN content delivery performance.
- [CDN timeouts](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/cdn-timeouts.md): Configure proxy_read_timeout, proxy_connect_timeout, proxy_send_timeout, and client_header_timeout directives for Gcore Edge Network CDN origin servers.
- [About CDN resources interface: how it is arranged](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/about-cdn-resources-interface-how-it-is-arranged.md): Navigate CDN resources interface: create resources, filter by status (Active, Suspended, In processing, Deleted), search by CNAME or ID, and access Settings,...
- [Add an origin group to a CDN resource](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/add-an-origin-group.md): Create and attach origin groups with single or multiple origins to CDN resources, configuring failover logic and round-robin distribution between primary and...

## CDN resource options

## General
- [Specify an origin and the origin pull protocol](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/general/specify-an-origin-and-the-origin-pull-protocol.md): Configure origin server address and select HTTP, HTTPS, or HTTP/HTTPS protocol for CDN content retrieval from origin servers.
- [Use a private bucket as an origin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/general/use-a-private-bucket-as-an-origin.md): Configure private S3 buckets as CDN origins using AWS Signature V4 authentication with Access Key ID, Secret Access Key, Hostname, and Region credentials.
- [Enable origin SSL validation](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/general/enable-origin-ssl-validation.md): Configure CDN origin SSL validation by uploading X.509 certificates via API and enabling proxy_ssl_enabled, proxy_ssl_ca, proxy_ssl_data parameters for certi...
- [Create and set a custom domain for the content delivery via CDN](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/general/create-and-set-a-custom-domain-for-the-content-delivery-via-cdn.md): Configure custom domains and CNAME records for CDN static content delivery; set up DNS aliases pointing to Gcore's gcdn.co endpoint.
- [Enable and configure origin shielding](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/general/enable-and-configure-origin-shielding.md): Enable origin shielding in Gcore CDN Resource settings to deploy a precache server between origin and CDN edge servers, reducing origin load during traffic b...
- [Suspend a CDN resource automatically or manually](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/general/suspend-a-cdn-resource-automatically-or-manually.md): Suspend CDN resources automatically after 90 days of inactivity or manually via Content availability toggle in resource settings.
- [Customize connection and read timeouts](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/general/customize-connection-and-read-timeouts.md): Configure custom connection and read timeouts for CDN resources to control edge node wait duration when connecting to origin servers and receiving responses.

## Cache
- [Specify cache lifetime on a CDN resource or origin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cache/specify-cache-lifetime-on-a-cdn-resource-or-origin.md): Configure Cache-Control header max-age and CDN caching lifetime via origin server directives or CDN control panel to control file retention duration.
- [Set up a CDN resource to follow origin redirects](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cache/set-up-a-cdn-resource-to-follow-origin-redirects.md): Enable CDN to follow origin HTTP 3xx redirects (301, 302, 303, 307, 308) via Portal or API with configurable status codes.
- [Specify cache lifetime for user browsers](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cache/specify-cache-lifetime-for-user-browsers.md): Configure browser cache lifetime using Origin-controlled Cache-Control headers or CDN-controlled max-age values via Gcore Customer Portal.
- [Ignore the set cookie or query string parameters when caching content on CDN servers](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cache/ignore-the-set-cookie-or-query-string-parameters-when-caching-content-on-cdn-servers.md): Configure CDN cache behavior for query string parameters using Ignore All, Ignore All Except, or Ignore Only modes to control cache key generation.
- [Always online: provide the stale cache if the origin is unavailable](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cache/always-online-provide-the-stale-cache-if-the-origin-is-unavailable.md): Serve stale cached content when origin is unavailable due to network errors, timeouts, or HTTP 5xx/4xx errors; configure error codes in Resource Advanced Set...
- [Сache Sharding: share cache between CDN servers](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cache/cache-sharding-share-cache-between-cdn-servers.md): Enable Cache Sharding to distribute cached content across CDN servers within a PoP using consistent hashing, reducing origin requests and improving cache hit...
- [Modify cache key](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cache/modify-cache-key.md): Configure cache key variables (URI, protocol, normalized URI) in CDN resource settings to control cache object identification and improve hit ratio.

## Access
- [Control access to the content with country, referrer, IP, and user agents policies](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/security/control-access-to-the-content-with-country-referrer-ip-and-user-agents-policies.md): Configure CDN resource access restrictions using country, referrer, IP address, and user agent policies via Portal or API.
- [Set up a redirect from HTTP to HTTPS](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/security/set-up-a-redirect-from-http-to-https.md): Enable HTTP to HTTPS redirect on CDN resources via API or Portal after configuring SSL certificates in General and Access sections.

## Secure token
- [About secure token](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/security/use-a-secure-token/about-secure-token.md): Protect CDN content from unauthorized access using Secure Token with IP allowlisting and URL expiration to restrict downloads by time or source IP address.
- [Configure and use secure token](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/security/use-a-secure-token/configure-and-use-secure-token.md): Enable secure token authentication with MD5 hash validation, IP allowlisting, and path-based access control for CDN content delivery.
- [Specify HTTP methods allowed for content requests from the CDN](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/security/specify-http-methods-allowed-for-content-requests-from-the-cdn.md): Configure allowed HTTP methods (GET, HEAD, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS) for CDN resources to block unsupported requests and return 405/501 responses.
- [Set the hostname passed in SNI requests to the origin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/security/set-the-hostname-passed-in-sni-requests-to-the-origin-server.md): Configure SNI hostname in HTTPS origin requests using dynamic or custom hostname modes in CDN resource security settings.
- [Specify TLS versions allowed for content requests from the CDN](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/security/choose-tls-versions.md): Configure allowed TLS protocol versions (SSLv3, TLSv1, TLSv1.1, TLSv1.2, TLSv1.3) for CDN content requests via Gcore Customer Portal.

## Security
- [Protect CDN resources with Gcore WAAP](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cdn-security/protect-cdn-resources-with-basic-waf.md): Enable WAAP Layer 7 DDoS and web application protection for CDN resources via Customer Portal Security settings with exception rules.

## Compression
- [Configure Gzip and Brotli compression](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/compression/configure-gzip-and-brotli-compression.md): Enable Gzip and Brotli edge compression on Gcore CDN with configurable MIME types; requires Origin Shielding for Brotli and Accept-Encoding header support.
- [Configure fetch compression](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/compression/configure-fetch-compression.md): Enable Fetch compressed option in CDN resource Content settings to pull pre-compressed content from origin servers supporting GZip compression.
- [Configuring HTTP status codes for CDN-hosted content](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/specify-custom-http-status-code-for-the-content-delivered-by-the-cdn.md): Configure custom HTTP status codes for CDN resources via Resource settings or Rules, with optional scheduling and redirect URLs.
- [Optimize large file delivery](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/optimize-large-file-delivery.md): Enable Large File Delivery Optimization in CDN Resource Settings to serve 10 MB+ content via chunked range requests with origin caching.
- [Rewrite: redirect requests from the CDN to the origin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/rewrite-redirect-requests-from-the-cdn-to-the-origin.md): Configure CDN Rewrite option with nginx regex directives to redirect origin requests from one path to another using capture groups and flags.
- [WebSockets: allow permanent connections with the origin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/websockets-allow-permanent-connections-with-the-origin.md): Enable or disable WebSocket protocol for persistent bidirectional TCP connections between CDN resources and origin servers.

## HTTP headers
- [Specify HTTP headers that CDN adds to requests to the origin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/http-headers/specify-http-headers-that-cdn-adds-to-requests-to-the-origin.md): Configure custom HTTP request headers that CDN appends to origin requests via Request Headers option in Customer Portal.
- [Add the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header to the browser response](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/http-headers/add-the-access-control-allow-origin-header-to-the-browser-response.md): Configure Access-Control-Allow-Origin CORS header in Gcore CDN resource settings or rules to allow cross-origin requests from specified domains or any origin.
- [Add or hide response headers](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/http-headers/add-or-hide-response-headers.md): Configure custom HTTP response headers in CDN resource settings with header name, value, status code filtering, and support for multiple values per header.
- [Configure and check the host header](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/http-headers/configure-and-check-the-host-header.md): Configure and check the Host header in CDN resources using Custom Host header or Forward Host header options to ensure correct origin requests.
- [Use the X-Forwarded-Proto header to identify the protocol used for CDN servers requests](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/http-headers/use-the-x-forwarded-proto-header-to-identify-the-protocol-used-for-cdn-servers-requests.md): Configure X-Forwarded-Proto header on CDN servers to identify client protocol (HTTP/HTTPS) for conditional redirects and content delivery logic.
- [Network limits: set the content delivery speed to end users](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/network-limits-set-the-content-delivery-speed-to-end-users.md): Configure CDN Download Speed Limit with Static mode (Connection Speed, Limit After parameters) or Dynamic mode (speed, buffer query arguments) to throttle co...

## Image optimization (paid)
- [About image stack](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/image-optimization-paid/about-image-stack.md): Optimize JPG and PNG images on-the-fly using Image Stack with quality adjustment, WebP/AVIF compression, resize, and crop operations cached in CDN.
- [Enable image stack](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/image-optimization-paid/enable-image-stack.md): Request account activation via Gcore Support, then enable Image Stack in CDN resource Rules with image quality, WebP/AVIF compression, and resize/crop parame...

## Image stack tools
- [Сonfigure image compression to WebP and AVIF](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/image-optimization-paid/image-stack-tools/configure-image-compression-to-webp-and-avif.md): Enable automatic WebP and AVIF compression for JPG and PNG images via CDN Image Stack rules or query string parameters.
- [Change image quality](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/image-optimization-paid/image-stack-tools/change-image-quality.md): Reduce image quality via Image Stack option using portal settings or query string parameters with preset or custom quality values.
- [Crop images](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/image-optimization-paid/image-stack-tools/crop-images.md): Configure CDN image cropping with query string parameters to automatically crop JPG/PNG images before delivery using aspect ratio, dimensions, or gravity set...
- [Reduce image size](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/image-optimization-paid/image-stack-tools/reduce-image-size.md): Configure CDN image resize using query parameters to reduce JPG/PNG dimensions via compression or cropping before delivery.
- [Possible image stack errors](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/image-optimization-paid/possible-image-stack-errors.md): Diagnose Image Stack processing failures using HTTP status codes, response headers (X-Img-Server, X-IMG-Saved-Bytes, X-IMG-Processing-Time), and developer to...
- [Query String Forwarding: forward parameters to sub-requests](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/query-string-forwarding.md): Forward query string parameters from HLS/MPEG-DASH master manifests to nested rendition manifests and segments via CDN resource configuration or API.
- [Determine in which regions the content will be delivered with Geobalancer for paid tariffs](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/determine-in-which-regions-the-content-will-be-delivered-with-geo-balancer-for-paid-tariffs.md): Configure Geobalancer to restrict content delivery to specific regions (North America, Latin America, Asia, Middle East, Australia, Africa, Europe) and set d...

## Rules for particular files
- [Create a rule manually or from a template to configure settings for particular files](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/rules-for-particular-files/create-a-rule-manually-or-from-a-template-to-configure-settings-for-particular-files.md): Create CDN rules for specific file paths with custom caching, origin protocol, and delivery options using manual creation or predefined templates.
- [Create a CDN resource rule template manually or use a system one](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/rules-for-particular-files/create-a-cdn-resource-rule-template-manually-or-use-a-system-one.md): Create custom CDN resource rule templates with file paths, origin pull protocols, and caching options, or apply pre-built System templates for video streamin...
- [Avoid indexing of your CDN resource by web crawlers](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/rules-for-particular-files/set-robots-txt-to-avoid-adding-to-index-by-web-crawlers.md): Prevent web crawler indexing of CDN custom domains using Status code rules to avoid duplicate content in search results and SEO degradation.
- [Clear CDN resource cache by URL, pattern, or all and check purge history](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/clear-cdn-resource-cache-by-url-pattern-or-all.md): Purge CDN resource cache by URL, pattern, or all using API or Gcore Customer Portal; check purge history and request limits.

- [How the CDN service and its additional options are billed](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/how-the-cdn-service-and-its-additional-options-are-billed.md): Calculate CDN service billing based on monthly traffic limits, overage charges, and request limits with per-unit pricing for exceeded thresholds.

## IP configuration options
- [Bring Your Own IP](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/ip-configuration-options/byoip.md): Announce custom IP subnets and ASNs from Gcore infrastructure or your own autonomous system for IP address management control.
- [Dedicated IP](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/ip-configuration-options/dedicated-ip.md): Provision dedicated IP addresses or subnets for isolated content delivery and enhanced security isolation from shared IP networks.
- [Load content to the CDN before users request it](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/load-the-content-to-cdn-before-users-request-it.md): Use Prefetch to preload files (200 MB–5 GB) into CDN cache before user requests via CDN Resource paths.

- [Log viewer: view and download CDN resource logs](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/logs/log-viewer-view-and-download-cdn-resource-logs.md): View and download CDN resource logs with filters for time, IP, HTTP method, status code, data center, and cache status; export up to 10,000 entries per 24-ho...

## SSL certificates
- [Add an SSL certificate to deliver content over HTTPS](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/ssl-certificates/add-an-ssl-certificate-to-deliver-content-over-https.md): Attach SSL certificates to CDN resources for HTTPS delivery using custom domains with self-managed or Let's Encrypt certificates.
- [Configure your own SSL certificate](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/ssl-certificates/configure-your-own-ssl-certificate.md): Upload custom SSL certificates in PEM format with certificate chain and private key to Gcore CDN, then attach to CDN resources for HTTPS delivery on custom d...
- [Configure Let's Encrypt certificate](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/ssl-certificates/configure-lets-encrypt-certificate.md): Enable HTTPS with free Let's Encrypt certificates on CDN resources using HTTP-01 or DNS-01 challenge validation methods.

## Terraform
- [Install Terraform and integrate it with Gcore CDN](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/terraform/install-and-configure-terraform.md): Install Terraform, configure Gcore provider with API token, and initialize Terraform modules for CDN resource management.
- [Create and manage CDN resources with Terraform](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/terraform/manage-a-cdn-resource-with-terraform.md): Configure Gcore CDN resources using Terraform provider with origin groups, active/backup origins, and upstream failover options via main.tf.
- [Copy CDN resources via Terraform](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/terraform/copy-cdn-resources-via-terraform.md): Duplicate CDN resources across environments using Terraform variables, workspaces, and .tfvars files with API token authentication.
- [Create a CDN rule via Terraform](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/terraform/create-cdn-rule-via-terraform.md): Add CDN rules to Terraform-managed resources using gcore_cdn_resource with rule blocks, path patterns, and optional compression settings.
- [Add an SSL certificate to a CDN resource via Terraform](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/terraform/add-ssl-certificate-to-cdn-via-terraform.md): Configure SSL certificate for CDN resource in Terraform using main.tf with certificate name, public key, and private key parameters.
- [Set up version control and roll back to previous configurations](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/terraform/version-control-and-rollback-via-terraform.md): Integrate Terraform and Git for CDN configuration versioning, rollback procedures, and infrastructure change management with commit history and tagged releases.

## Troubleshooting
- [Common Setup Issues](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/common-setup-issues.md): Diagnose and resolve CDN setup issues including 503 errors, redirect loops, MIME type problems, and SSL certificate validation failures.
- [Resolve errors by status code for GCore CDN](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/gcore-error-status-codes.md): Diagnose CDN delivery errors using status codes 403 and 404 with Log viewer, identify misconfiguration causes (disabled resources, access restrictions, origi...
- [Traceparent header for troubleshooting](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/traceparent-header-for-troubleshooting.md): Implement W3C traceparent HTTP header for distributed tracing across Gcore Edge Network with trace-id and parent-id fields in requests and raw logs.
- [Content is unavailable after a CDN resource creation: how to solve the issue](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/content-is-unavailable-after-a-cdn-resource-creation-how-to-solve-the-issue.md): Diagnose and resolve CDN content delivery failures by verifying origin HTTP responses, validating CNAME DNS configuration, and checking Host Header settings.
- [Cache hit ratio is low: How to solve the issue](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/cache-percentage-is-low-how-to-solve-the-issue.md): Diagnose and resolve low cache hit ratio issues using Statistics metrics, response headers, and cache-control directives in Gcore CDN.
- [Content delivery speed is low: how to solve the issue](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/content-delivery-speed-is-low-how-to-solve-the-issue.md): Diagnose and resolve low CDN content delivery speed by comparing origin and CDN performance, checking cache hit ratio, enabling large file optimization, and ...
- [An SSL certificate isn't working: how to solve the issue](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/an-ssl-certificate-isn-t-working-how-to-solve-to-issue.md): Troubleshoot SSL certificate attachment failures by verifying certificate chain formatting and Let's Encrypt issuance by checking ACME challenge path rules a...
- [3xx error: how to solve CDN issues with redirect](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/3xx-error-how-to-solve-cdn-issues-with-redirect.md): Resolve CDN 3xx redirect errors by configuring origin pull protocol, Host Header, and origin domain settings to match redirect behavior.
- [4xx error: how to solve CDN issues with the limited access and content non-existence](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/4xx-error-how-to-solve-cdn-issues-with-the-limited-access-and-content-non-existence.md): Diagnose and resolve 4xx CDN errors by validating origin accessibility, allowlisting CDN edge IPs, configuring Origin Pull Protocol, and verifying Host Heade...
- [5xx error: how to solve server issues](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/5xx-error-how-to-solve-server-issues.md): Diagnose and resolve HTTP 5xx errors on CDN edge servers by checking incidents, origin server availability, IP whitelisting, origin pull protocol, and Host H...

## FastEdge

Serverless WebAssembly applications at edge for HTTP and CDN extensions; Rust SDK, JavaScript SDK, Proxy-Wasm; deploy and manage apps, templates, Secrets Manager with slot-based rotation, Edge Storage (key-value, Sorted Set, Bloom Filter); FastEdge CLI for local development; troubleshooting via status codes 530, 531, 532, 533.

- [FastEdge](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge.md)
- [FastEdge overview](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/getting-started.md): Deploy WebAssembly functions on Gcore's edge network for serverless execution with HTTP request/response modification, A/B testing, personalization, and CDN ...

## Create apps
- [Create FastEdge apps](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/create-apps.md): Deploy HTTP and CDN FastEdge applications using Rust SDK, JavaScript SDK, or Proxy-Wasm on Gcore's serverless CDN network.
- [Create FastEdge HTTP applications](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/getting-started/create-fastedge-apps.md): Build FastEdge HTTP applications using Rust or JavaScript SDKs to compile WebAssembly binaries, then deploy via Customer Portal or API.
- [Extend CDN functionality with FastEdge](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-fastedge.md): Integrate FastEdge serverless compute with CDN edge nodes to extend content delivery with custom logic and request/response manipulation.
- [CDN properties](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/getting-started/cdn-properties.md): Access and modify HTTP request/response properties via Proxy-Wasm ABI hooks using read/write methods; properties include URL path, headers, geolocation data,...
- [Manage FastEdge apps](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/manage-apps.md): View, start, stop, and configure FastEdge HTTP and CDN applications; manage environment variables, secrets, Edge Storage, response headers, metrics, and appl...
- [Create and manage templates](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/create-manage-templates.md): Create FastEdge templates with custom binaries, parameters, secrets, and environment variables for HTTP and CDN applications via Customer Portal.

## Secrets Manager
- [Secrets Manager](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/secrets-manager/manage-secrets.md): Store and manage encrypted secrets across FastEdge applications using Secrets Manager with create, edit, delete, and slot-based access operations.
- [Secrets Slots](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/secrets-manager/slots.md): Implement secret rotation using Slots mechanism to access versioned encrypted secrets by index or timestamp without deleting old values.

## Edge Storage
- [How it works](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/kv-stores/how-it-works.md): Deploy distributed key-value storage instances co-located with FastEdge WASM runtime at every edge location for sub-millisecond read latency and eventual con...
- [Edge Storage](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/kv-stores/manage-kv-store.md): Create and manage key-value pairs in Edge Storage across Gcore's global edge network with insert, edit, delete, and file upload operations via FastEdge portal.
- [Sorted Set](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/kv-stores/sorted-set.md): Create, edit, and delete Sorted Sets (ZSET) in Gcore FastEdge Edge Storage with score-ordered members for leaderboards and rankings.
- [Bloom Filter](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/kv-stores/bloom-filter.md): Create and manage Bloom Filters in FastEdge Edge Storage via Gcore Customer Portal for space-efficient membership testing with configurable hash functions an...
- [Troubleshooting](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/troubleshooting.md): Diagnose FastEdge application failures using HTTP status codes (530, 531, 532, 533) for memory limits, timeouts, crashes, and internal errors.
- [FastEdge CLI](https://gcore.com/docs/fastedge/fastedge-cli.md): Install, build, and execute FastEdge HTTP applications locally using FastEdge CLI with Rust, including environment variables, geo headers, and request testin...

## Edge Cloud

Virtual Machines (Standard, GPU, CPU, Memory, High-frequency, Basic, TPM, Network-optimized) and Bare Metal servers across global regions; networking with private/public/dedicated networks, subnets, routers, floating/reserved/virtual IPs, BYOIP; Load Balancers with TLS, mTLS, SNI, Prometheus monitoring; NFS file shares; Managed Kubernetes on VM or Bare Metal with autoscaling and OpenID Connect; volumes (High IOPS SSD, Standard, SSD Low-Latency), snapshots, images; advanced DDoS protection for Bare Metal; placement groups; projects, user roles, quotas, tags, billing.

- [Cloud](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud.md)
- [About Gcore cloud](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/about-gcore-cloud.md): Access Gcore Edge Cloud IaaS and PaaS services with on-demand resource delivery, pay-as-you-go pricing, and TIER III/IV data centers in Manassas, Amsterdam, ...
- [Billing](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/billing.md): Track Edge Cloud PAYG billing per-minute charges, balance thresholds (€4/$4), payment methods, service suspension policies, and resource-specific pricing for...

- [View user actions](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/getting-started/projects/users/view-user-actions.md): View user action logs in Gcore Edge Cloud with filters for resource ID, project, action type, resource group, and time range.
- [Cost reports](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/getting-started/view-statistics-on-expenses.md): View Cloud resource usage costs filtered by project, region, resource type, and consumption period using Pay-As-You-Go pricing model via Cost Report page or ...

- [Create and manage a router](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/create-and-manage-a-router.md): Create and configure Cloud Router instances with static routes, SNAT, and subnet associations for dynamic route exchange between networks.

## IP address
- [Create and configure a floating IP address](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/ip-address/create-and-configure-a-floating-ip-address.md): Assign static public IP addresses to VMs, Bare Metal servers, and Load Balancers using floating IPs with subnet routing configuration and traffic forwarding.
- [Create and configure a reserved IP address](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/ip-address/create-and-configure-a-reserved-ip-address.md): Allocate dedicated public, private, or dedicated public IP addresses to Virtual Machines, Bare Metal servers, or Load Balancers with optional custom IPv4/IPv...
- [Create and configure a virtual IP address](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/ip-address/create-and-configure-a-virtual-ip-vip-address.md): Reserve and assign virtual IP addresses (VIP) to VMs using Keepalived and VRRP for fault tolerance or as secondary network interface addresses.
- [Difference between virtual, floating, and reserved IP addresses](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/ip-address/difference-between-virtual-floating-and-reserved-ip-addresses.md): Compare Floating IP, Reserved IP, and Virtual IP address types in Gcore Cloud: DNAT mapping, pre-allocation, shared failover, assignment scope, billing, and ...
- [Configure an allowed address pair](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/ip-address/configure-an-allowed-address-pair.md): Assign additional IP addresses to VM network interfaces using the allowed address pairs API endpoint with MAC-IP binding configuration.

## Load balancers
- [Create and configure a load balancer](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/create-and-configure-a-load-balancer.md): Configure Load Balancer region, computing resources (vCPU/GiB), network type (Public/Private/Dedicated), IP family (IPv4/IPv6/Dual), reserved IP assignment, ...
- [Add TLS certificates to a load balancer](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/load-balancers/add-certificates-to-load-balancer.md): Configure multiple TLS certificates on Load Balancer listeners using SNI to serve different domains on a single IP address via Terminated HTTPS or Prometheus...
- [Configure mutual TLS authentication](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/load-balancers/configure-mtls-authentication.md): Configure mutual TLS authentication between Gcore Load Balancers and backend servers using PKCS12/PEM certificates, CA verification, and mTLS pool parameters.
- [Manage load balancers](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/load-balancers/manage-load-balancers.md): View Load Balancer properties, statuses, listeners, IP addresses, regions, and tags in Customer Portal; monitor operating and provisioning status of Load Bal...
- [Logging for load balancers](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/load-balancers/logging-for-load-balancers.md): Configure Logging for Load Balancers with namespace and topic creation, including guaranteed throughput metrics across region pairs.
- [Use annotations for load balancers](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/load-balancers/use-annotations-for-load-balancers.md): Configure Load Balancer behavior in Gcore Managed Kubernetes using annotations for network type, flavor, IP assignment, listener timeouts, and pool node sele...
- [Monitoring for load balancers](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/load-balancers/monitoring-for-load-balancers.md): Configure Prometheus listeners on load balancers to expose metrics endpoints for scraping with Prometheus and Grafana visualization.
- [Add and configure a firewall](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/add-and-configure-a-firewall.md): Create firewall rules with inbound/outbound traffic policies, template rules (SSH/HTTP/HTTPS/MySQL/DNS/PostgreSQL/RDP), custom protocols/ports, CIDR sources,...
- [Bring Your Own IP (BYOIP)](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/networking/byoip.md): Configure custom public IP address ranges with Gcore using BYOIP, requiring LOA, IRR Route Object, RPKI ROA, and CIDR prefix for IP ownership preservation an...

## File shares
- [About file shares](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/file-shares/about-file-shares.md): Create and manage NFS file shares across VMs, Bare Metal, and Kubernetes pods with configurable access rules and snapshot recovery.
- [Configuring file shares](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/file-shares/configure-file-shares.md): Create NFS-based file shares (Standard or VAST) and mount them to Linux VMs, bare metal servers, or GPU clusters with network and access configuration.
- [Migrate data from S3 and EFS to Gcore](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/file-shares/how-to-migrate-data-from-s3.md): Migrate data from AWS S3 and EFS to Gcore Object Storage or File Shares using rclone with S3-compatible APIs and NFS mounts.

- [Bare Metal Kubernetes](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/bare-metal-kubernetes.md): Deploy Kubernetes worker nodes on Bare Metal servers with Managed Kubernetes for direct hardware access, eliminating virtualization overhead and improving pe...

## Clusters
- [Create a Kubernetes cluster](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/create-a-kubernetes-cluster.md): Configure Kubernetes cluster region, version, node pools with VM/Bare Metal flavors, disk types (High IOPS SSD, Standard, SSD Low Latency), autoscaling min/m...
- [Upgrade a Kubernetes cluster](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/upgrade.md): Upgrade Kubernetes cluster version using rolling updates with API compatibility checks and downgrade support for one patch version.

## Connect
- [Connect to your nodes via SSH](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/connect/connect-to-your-nodes-via-ssh-using-a-floating-ip-address.md): Assign floating IP addresses to Kubernetes nodes and connect via SSH using floating IP and private key authentication.
- [Install kubectl and connect to a Kubernetes cluster](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/connect/install-kubectl-and-connect-to-a-kubernetes-cluster.md): Install kubectl binary on Windows, configure PATH environment variable, and set up kubeconfig file in .kube directory for cluster access.
- [Secure Kubernetes cluster with OpenID Connect](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/secure-cluster-with-oidc.md): Configure OpenID Connect authentication for Kubernetes clusters with issuer URL, client ID, groups claim, signing algorithms, and username claim settings.

## Autoscaling
- [About autoscaling](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/autoscaling/about-autoscaling.md): Configure Kubernetes node pool autoscaling with minimum/maximum limits to automatically scale nodes based on pod resource requests and idle time thresholds.
- [Change autoscaling limits](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/autoscaling/change-autoscaling-limits.md): Modify minimum and maximum node counts for Kubernetes cluster autoscaling pools via Cloud console Pools section.
- [Create and configure a pool](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/autoscaling/create-a-new-pool-in-cluster.md): Create Kubernetes cluster pools with node configuration, autoscaling limits, VM/Bare Metal selection, placement policies, labels, taints, and autohealing set...
- [Advanced cluster autoscaler settings](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/autoscaling/advanced-cluster-autoscaler-settings.md): Configure Kubernetes cluster autoscaler parameters: scan interval, expander strategy (random, most-pods, least-waste, price, priority, gRPC), node provision ...
- [Configure GPU autoscaling for Kubernetes](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/autoscaling/configure-gpu-autoscaling-for-kubernetes.md): Install and configure KEDA for GPU-based autoscaling in Gcore Managed Kubernetes clusters with Bare Metal worker pools.
- [Add users with limited rights to a Kubernetes cluster](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/clusters/add-users-with-limited-rights-to-a-kubernetes-cluster.md): Create Kubernetes service accounts and namespaces with kubectl to grant users restricted API access via kubeconfig files scoped to specific namespaces.
- [Monitor load on a specific node](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/monitor-load-on-a-specific-node.md): Monitor CPU Utilization, RAM Utilization, Network Traffic, and Network Packets metrics for Kubernetes nodes via Monitoring tab in Cloud console.

- [Install nginx Ingress controller](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/networking/install-and-set-up-the-nginx-ingress-controller.md): Deploy nginx Ingress Controller on Managed Kubernetes using Helm, automatically provisioning a Load Balancer for traffic routing to services.
- [Use Nginx Ingress Controller](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/networking/use-the-nginx-ingress-controller.md): Deploy Nginx Ingress Controller using kubectl to create Ingress objects with ingressClassResource, Service routing, and port configuration.

## Storage
- [Create a PVC and bind to a pod](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/storage/create-a-pvc-and-bind-it-to-a-pod.md): Create PersistentVolumeClaim (PVC) with StorageClass for standard, ssd_hiiops, or ssd_lowlatency disk types and bind to Kubernetes pods.
- [Configure NFS CSI driver for Managed Kubernetes](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/kubernetes/storage/configure-nfs-csi-driver-for-kubernetes.md): Configure NFS CSI driver to provision persistent volumes in Managed Kubernetes clusters using Gcore File Shares with dynamic attachment and management.

## Managed PostgreSQL
- [About managed PostgreSQL](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/managed-database-postgresql.md): Deploy and manage PostgreSQL 13/14/15 relational database instances with configurable flavor, storage, replication mode, and IP access control lists.
- [Manage a PostgreSQL server](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/managed-database-postgresql/manage-postgresql-servers.md): Create PostgreSQL servers with region, version, compute flavor, storage volume, and optional high-availability replication modes (asynchronous/synchronous).

## Install a log shipper
- [Install and configure Filebeat](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/logging-as-a-service/install-a-log-shipper/install-and-configure-filebeat.md): Install Filebeat and configure inputs, output.kafka, Kafka endpoint, SASL authentication, and SSL encryption for log shipping to Gcore Managed Logging.
- [Install and configure Fluent Bit](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/logging-as-a-service/install-a-log-shipper/install-and-configure-fluent-bit.md): Configure Fluent Bit [INPUT] and [OUTPUT] sections with log source, Kafka brokers, SASL authentication, and Gcore Logging endpoint to ship logs to OpenSearch...
- [Install and configure Fluentd](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/logging-as-a-service/install-a-log-shipper/install-and-configure-fluentd.md): Configure Fluentd with Kafka output plugin to stream logs to Gcore Managed Logging using brokers, SASL authentication, and topic settings.
- [Install and configure Logstash](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/logging-as-a-service/install-a-log-shipper/install-and-configure-logstash.md): Install Logstash and configure Kafka output plugin with SASL authentication to send logs to Gcore Managed Logging.
- [Prometheus exporter](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/logging-as-a-service/prometheus-exporter.md): Export OpenSearch index metrics via Prometheus exporter endpoint using HTTPS Basic Authentication with Managed Logging credentials and gauge metrics.
- [View GCore managed logging logs in Grafana with OpenSearch plugin](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/logging-as-a-service/view-opensearch-logs-in-Grafana.md): Integrate Gcore Managed Logging with Grafana using OpenSearch plugin for consolidated log visualization and alerting.
- [Manage cloud via Terraform](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/manage-cloud-via-terraform.md): Configure Gcore Cloud resources using Terraform provider with API tokens, main.tf configuration files, and resource definitions for Virtual Machines, Kuberne...
- [Manage cloud resources via Ansible](https://gcore.com/docs/cloud/manage-cloud-via-ansible.md): Install and configure Ansible with gcore/cloud collection to provision, deploy, and orchestrate Gcore Cloud resources via playbooks and modules.

## Edge AI

GPU clusters (Bare Metal, Spot Bare Metal, Virtual GPU) with H200, H100, A100, L40S nodes and InfiniBand or Ethernet networking for LLM training and distributed AI/ML workloads; Everywhere Inference for deploying trained models on edge with anycast endpoints and smart routing, Docker containers, vLLM, Diffusers, container image registries, API keys, and quotas.

- [Edge AI](https://gcore.com/docs/edge-ai.md)
- [Getting started](https://gcore.com/docs/edge-ai/getting-started.md): Set up GPU clusters for AI model training or deploy models for inference using Gcore GPU Cloud and Everywhere Inference via Portal or API.
- [Billing](https://gcore.com/docs/edge-ai/billing.md): Track GPU Virtual Machine and Bare Metal charges, Everywhere Inference per-minute compute billing, and monthly resource fee processing with payment failure s...

## GPU cloud
- [About GPU Cloud](https://gcore.com/docs/edge-ai/ai-infrastructure/about-gpu-cloud.md): Deploy GPU clusters with Bare Metal, Spot Bare Metal, or Virtual GPU instances; configure H200/H100/A100/L40S nodes with InfiniBand or Ethernet for LLM train...

- [Manage a Virtual GPU cluster](https://gcore.com/docs/edge-ai/ai-infrastructure/manage-a-virtual-gpu-cluster.md): Monitor Virtual GPU cluster nodes, manage power state (power on/off, reboot), configure volumes and network interfaces, and delete cluster resources via Gcor...
- [Create a Bare Metal GPU cluster](https://gcore.com/docs/edge-ai/ai-infrastructure/create-a-bare-metal-gpu-cluster.md): Create Bare Metal GPU clusters with dedicated physical servers, configurable node count, GPU models, and InfiniBand networking for distributed AI/ML training...
- [Manage a Bare Metal GPU cluster](https://gcore.com/docs/edge-ai/ai-infrastructure/manage-a-bare-metal-gpu-cluster.md): Monitor and manage Bare Metal GPU cluster nodes, resize clusters by adding/removing nodes, configure network interfaces, manage power states, and delete clus...
- [Spot Bare Metal GPU](https://gcore.com/docs/edge-ai/ai-infrastructure/spot-bare-metal-gpu.md): Deploy discounted Spot Bare Metal GPU clusters with 24-hour reclamation notice for interruptible batch processing, experiments, and development workloads.

## Managed DNS

Authoritative DNS zones with AnycastDNS, DNSSEC, and healthchecks for failover; managed records A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, SRV, CAA, PTR, SOA, SPF, and HTTPS; zone import and export in BIND format, CNAME flattening at zone apex; dynamic responses with GeoDistance, Weighted shuffle, ASN, Country, Continent, Region, and IP pickers; CDN integration; Terraform provider, OctoDNS secondary DNS, Let's Encrypt issuance via Certbot.

- [Managed DNS](https://gcore.com/docs/dns.md)
- [GCore DNS overview](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/about-gcore-dns.md): Configure authoritative DNS zones with Managed DNS using AnycastDNS, geo-balancing, DNSSEC, health checks, and third-party integrations via Portal or API.
- [GCore managed DNS glossary](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/gcore-dns-glossary.md): Define DNS terminology including A/AAAA/CNAME records, DNS zones, DNSSEC, DNS propagation, authoritative nameservers, and DNS queries used in Gcore Managed DNS.
- [Getting started with managed DNS](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/manage-a-dns-zone.md): Create DNS zones in Gcore Customer Portal or API, configure NS records, and manage DNS records with TTL settings via the managed DNS infrastructure.

## Records
- [Supported DNS record types](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/dns-records.md): Reference eleven supported DNS record types in Gcore Managed DNS: A, AAAA, NS, CNAME, MX, TXT, SRV, CAA, PTR, SOA, and SPF with TTL and class parameters.
- [Manage DNS records](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/dns-records/manage-dns-records-non-advanced-interface-mode.md): Create and manage DNS records (A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, CAA, NS, SRV, HTTPS) in Gcore Customer Portal with TTL configuration and wildcard support.
- [Import and export records](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/dns-records/import-and-export-records.md): Export DNS records to BIND zone files (.zone) or import records from zone files to migrate domains and manage DNS record sets.
- [Create records in zone apex (CNAME flattening)](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/dns-records/specify-cname-at-root.md): Configure CNAME flattening in Gcore Managed DNS to create CNAME records at zone apex by removing A records and resolving CNAME chains to IP addresses.
- [Configure DNS records to send emails](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/dns-records/set-up-dns-for-sending-email.md): Configure A, MX, PTR, and SPF DNS records in Gcore Managed DNS to enable mail server authentication and prevent email spoofing and spam.
- [Setting up a PTR record and reverse DNS zone](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/dns-records/set-up-a-ptr-record-and-reverse-dns-zone.md): Configure PTR records and reverse DNS zones (in-addr.arpa, ip6.arpa) for IPv4/IPv6 addresses using Gcore Managed DNS with RIR delegation.
- [What is an HTTPS record and how is it configured?](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/dns-records/what-is-an-https-record-and-how-is-it-configured.md): Configure HTTPS DNS records in Gcore Managed DNS to reduce TLS handshake round trips and communicate protocol versions, ports, and alternate servers via sing...

## Advanced managing
- [Dynamic response](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/dns-records/configure-weight-balancing-and-geobalancing.md): Configure DNS dynamic response using pickers (GeoDistance, Weighted shuffle, ASN, Country, Continent, Region, IP) to deliver different records based on query...
- [What Healthcheck are and how to configure them](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/dns-failover/configure-and-use-dns-failover.md): Configure DNS Healthchecks to monitor server availability, exclude failed IPs from DNS responses, and redirect traffic to working servers using round-robin o...
- [Configure CDN integration](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/dns-records/advanced-managing/cdn-integration.md): Configure A/AAAA DNS records to resolve directly to Gcore CDN addresses, eliminating CNAME configuration and enabling automatic routing to optimal CDN endpoi...
- [Getting started with DNSSEC](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/getting-started-with-dnssec.md): Enable DNSSEC for DNS zones using API or Control Panel, configure DS records with registrars, and validate digital signatures across the DNS chain of trust.

## Integrations
- [Use GCore managed DNS as a secondary DNS with OctoDNS](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/dns-plugins/use-gcore-dns-as-a-secondary-dns-with-octodns.md): Configure Gcore managed DNS as secondary DNS using OctoDNS to synchronize zone records from primary DNS providers via Python virtualenv and YAML configuration.
- [Get a Let's Encrypt certificate with Certbot](https://gcore.com/docs/dns/dns-plugins/get-a-let-s-encrypt-certificate-with-certbot.md): Automate Let's Encrypt certificate issuance using Certbot plugin with Gcore DNS API and DNS-01 challenge validation.

## Hosting

Dedicated physical servers (Intel Xeon E3) and KVM virtual servers (KVM-SSD, KVM-SAS, KVM-HIGHCPU); manage via DCImanager, IPMI, and VMmanager 6 control panels; install Linux (CentOS, Debian, Ubuntu, FreeBSD) or Windows Server from templates or custom ISO; connect via RDP, SSH, or VNC; additional IPv4/IPv6 addresses, VLAN, 10 Gbps NICs, PTR records; statistics; troubleshooting suspended servers, ping and packet loss, IP blacklists, ISO installation, IPMI, SSH connectivity.

- [Hosting](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting.md)

## Dedicated servers
- [About dedicated server](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/about-dedicated-servers.md): Provision physical dedicated servers with Intel Xeon E3-1270 v5 processors, DDR4 ECC memory, IPMI management, and full resource allocation for data-intensive...

## Before purchase
- [Test the connectivity between your location and the server you want to buy](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/before-purchase/test-the-connectivity-between-your-location-and-the-server-you-want-to-buy.md): Use Looking Glass tool to test ping, traceroute, and BGP routes between your IP address and Gcore static nodes across locations.
- [Order a dedicated server](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/order-a-dedicated-server.md): Purchase dedicated servers by selecting tariffs, configuring parameters (OS, domain, traffic, IPv4/IPv6 addresses), and completing payment through Control Pa...
- [Upgrade your dedicated server](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/upgrade-your-dedicated-server.md): Request hardware upgrades for Dedicated Servers including RAID configuration, disk/RAM/network card changes via support tickets with 150 USD/EUR service fee.

## Manage
- [Log in to DCImanager](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/manage/log-in-to-dcimanager.md): Access DCImanager panel with dedicated credentials to manage servers, reboot, or stop instances via the Servers menu.
- [Log in to IPMI](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/manage/log-in-to-ipmi.md): Access IPMI interface for Dedicated Servers to remotely power on/off, reboot, or reinstall OS via DCI manager or server panel.

## Connect
- [Connect to a Windows server](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/manage/connect/connect-to-a-windows-server.md): Connect to Windows servers using Remote Desktop Connection with server IP address, username, and password from the control panel or purchase email.
- [Connect to a Linux server](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/manage/connect/connect-to-a-linux-server.md): Connect to Linux servers via SSH using server IP, username, and password, or access DCI Manager and VM Manager control panels with separate credentials.
- [Manage SSH keys](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/connect/linux-server/manage-ssh-keys.md): Create, edit, and delete SSH public keys for automated server authentication across dedicated server instances.
- [Change the port for SSH connections](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/connect/linux-server/change-the-port-for-ssh-connections.md): Modify SSH port in sshd_config, restart SSH service, update firewall rules, and verify connectivity on Linux servers.

## Operating system
- [Install a Linux OS from your ISO image](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/manage/operating-system/install-a-linux-os-from-your-iso-image.md): Upload and mount custom Linux ISO images to dedicated servers via DCI manager and IPMI Virtual Storage for OS installation.
- [Install a Linux OS from a template](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/manage/operating-system/install-a-linux-os-from-a-template.md): Deploy Linux OS templates (CentOS, Debian, Ubuntu, FreeBSD) or Windows Server to dedicated servers via DCI manager Operations panel.

## Networking

## Additional IP addresses
- [Buy an additional IP address](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/manage/networking/additional-ip-addresses/buy-an-additional-ip-address.md): Purchase additional IPv4 and IPv6 addresses for Dedicated Servers and Virtual Private Servers via Control Panel IP addresses management.
- [Configure an additional IP address](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/networking/additional-ip-addresses/configure-an-additional-ip-address.md): Configure additional IPv4 and IPv6 addresses on Debian/Ubuntu, CentOS, and Windows Server 2012 by editing network configuration files or Server Manager setti...
- [Connect dedicated servers into a VLAN](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/manage/networking/connect-dedicated-servers-into-a-vlan.md): Create and configure VLANs in DCI Manager to connect dedicated servers across physical locations with internal networking.
- [Configure a 10 Gbps network card](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/manage/networking/configure-a-10-gbps-network-card.md): Enable 10 Gbps network interface on Dedicated Servers using ethtool, ip commands, and network configuration files for Ubuntu/Debian with LACP support.
- [Set up a PTR record](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/set-up-a-ptr-record.md): Configure PTR records for VPS and Dedicated Server IP addresses to enable reverse DNS mapping for email server authentication.
- [Reboot a server](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/manage/reboot-a-server.md): Reboot a dedicated server instance via the Servers menu in the control panel.
- [Check statistics of your dedicated server](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/check-statistics-of-your-dedicated-server.md): View incoming and outgoing traffic statistics and total traffic volume for dedicated servers in DCI Manager Statistics section.
- [Delete a dedicated server](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/delete-a-dedicated-server.md): Remove dedicated server instances via Control Panel; permanently erases data and stops billing after deletion confirmation.

## Troubleshooting
- [Renew your server](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-a-server-that-was-suspended-for-non-payment.md): Recharge account balance or enable auto-payment to reactivate suspended virtual servers and resolve non-payment status.
- [Troubleshoot a server that is not responding to ping requests](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-a-server-that-is-not-responding-to-ping-requests.md): Diagnose ping connectivity failures by checking Great Firewall blocks, verifying network configuration (IP address, netmask, gateway), and confirming additio...
- [Remove your IP address from blacklists](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/troubleshooting/remove-your-ip-address-from-blacklists.md): Check IP blacklist status using third-party services and contact blacklist operators for delisting; configure DNS records and email authentication to prevent...
- [Troubleshoot errors with ISO image installation](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-errors-with-iso-image-installation.md): Diagnose ISO image upload failures, file naming restrictions, size limits, and DCI manager errors in dedicated server installations.
- [Troubleshoot IPMI errors](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-ipmi-errors.md): Diagnose and resolve IPMI login failures, session errors (code 1006), unavailable IPMI URLs, and remote console connection issues in DCImanager.
- [Troubleshoot issues with an incorrect IP location](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-issues-with-an-incorrect-ip-location.md): Verify IP geolocation accuracy using MaxMind and RIPE databases; contact support to request location updates if discrepancies persist.
- [Troubleshoot issues with SSH connection](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-issues-with-ssh-connection.md): Verify SSH port 22 connectivity using telnet or Putty, validate server credentials from Control Panel Instructions, and check OS settings via VNC/IPMI if con...
- [Troubleshoot packet loss and high ping](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-packet-loss-or-high-ping.md): Diagnose packet loss and latency exceeding 110ms using ping, tracert/traceroute/mtr commands; disable VPN/proxy or reset network settings via OS reinstall.

## Virtual servers
- [About virtual servers](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/about-virtual-servers.md): Configure KVM-SSD virtual servers with SSH access, VNC console, and isolated OS kernels on Debian, CentOS, Ubuntu, FreeBSD, Windows Server, or Solaris.

## Before purchase
- [Choose a virtual server for your needs](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/before-purchase/choose-a-virtual-server-for-your-needs.md): Compare KVM-SSD, KVM-SAS, KVM-HIGHCPU-SSD, KVM-HIGHCPU-SAS Virtual Server plans by CPU cores, RAM, disk type, data center location, and OS compatibility.
- [Test the connectivity between your location and the server you want to buy](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/before-purchase/test-the-connectivity-between-your-location-and-the-server-you-want-to-buy.md): Use Looking Glass tool to test ping, traceroute, and BGP routes between your IP address and Gcore static nodes across locations.
- [Order a virtual server](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/order-a-virtual-server.md): Purchase virtual server with OS selection, additional IP addresses, domain configuration, and automatic activation within 3 hours via cart checkout and payment.
- [Upgrade your virtual server](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/upgrade-your-virtual-server.md): Replace Virtual Server plan by selecting new tariff with matching location and higher or equal specifications, then submit upgrade request via Support ticket...

## Manage
- [Features and functions of VMmanager 6](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/main-features-and-functions-of-vmmanager-6.md): Manage VPS instances via VMmanager 6 control panel: start/stop/restart servers, reinstall OS, mount ISO images, manage disks, access VNC console, run scripts...

## Connect
- [Connect to a Windows server](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/dedicated-servers/manage/connect/connect-to-a-windows-server.md): Connect to Windows servers using Remote Desktop Connection with server IP address, username, and password from the control panel or purchase email.

## Linux server
- [Connect to a Linux server via control panel](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/connect/linux-server/connect-to-a-linux-server-via-control-panel.md): Access Linux server console through VMmanager control panel using the monitor icon for remote server management.
- [Connect to a Linux server via SSH](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/connect/linux-server/connect-to-linux-server-via-ssh.md): Establish SSH remote connections to Linux servers (CentOS, Debian, Ubuntu, FreeBSD) using credentials from server instructions panel.
- [Manage SSH keys](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/connect/linux-server/manage-ssh-keys.md): Create, edit, and delete SSH public keys for automated server authentication across dedicated server instances.
- [Change the port for SSH connections](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/connect/linux-server/change-the-port-for-ssh-connections.md): Modify SSH port in sshd_config, restart SSH service, update firewall rules, and verify connectivity on Linux servers.
- [Connect to a virtual server via VNC in VMmanager 6](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/connect/connect-to-a-virtual-server-via-vnc.md): Access VPS desktop via VNC console in VMmanager 6 using Remote Frame Buffer protocol with root credentials from Instructions tab.
- [Install GUI (desktop environment) on Ubuntu, CentOS and Debian](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/install-gui-desktop-environment-on-ubuntu-centos-and-debian.md): Install XFCE desktop environment on Ubuntu, CentOS, and Debian servers via apt/yum package managers and configure lightdm or xinit display manager.

## Operating system
- [Buy a Windows server](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/operating-system/buy-a-windows-server.md): Purchase Windows Server VPS instances by selecting datacenter, plan, payment period, and Windows Server OS during checkout; unavailable for KVM-SSD-1/2/3 plans.
- [Install a Linux OS from a template](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/operating-system/install-a-linux-os-from-a-template.md): Reinstall Linux OS on virtual servers using VM Manager templates including CentOS, Debian, FreeBSD, and Ubuntu distributions.
- [Install a Linux OS from your ISO image](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/operating-system/install-a-linux-os-from-your-iso-image.md): Boot a VPS from custom ISO image via Disks menu, set boot priority, and access installation through VNC console.
- [Install an operating system from a template in VMmanager 6](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/operating-system/install-an-os-from-a-template-in-vmmanager-6.md): Reinstall virtual machine operating systems from CentOS, Debian, FreeBSD, or Ubuntu templates in VMmanager 6 with optional application scripts and password c...
- [Install OS from ISO on in VMmanager 6](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/manage/operating-system/install-an-os-from-iso-in-vmmanager-6.md): Mount ISO images to VMmanager 6 virtual servers via HTTP(S) or FTP URLs with optional OS tags for script execution.

- [Delete a virtual server](https://gcore.com/docs/hosting/virtual-servers/delete-a-virtual-server.md): Remove virtual servers from Control Panel; permanently deletes data and stops billing after minimum 1-month service term.
